name: Òrrius and Dolmen de Can Buquet - circular through the Serralada Litoral park
name_original: Òrrius y Dolmen de Can Buquet - circular por el parque de la Serralada Litoral
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vilanova Del Vallès offers diverse landscapes, situated between the Valles plain and the foothills of the Litoral Mountain Range. A significant portion of the municipality is within the Serralada Litoral Park, featuring wooded slopes with white pines and holm oaks. The Mogent River basin provides flatter routes, complementing the undulating terrain of the hills. This area presents varied environments for cyclists, transitioning between forests and agricultural lands. Best touring cycling routes around Vilanova Del Vallès The most popular touring cycling route is Òrrius and Dolmen de Can Buquet - circular through the Serralada Litoral park, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the varied terrain of the Serralada Litoral Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Òrrius – Òrrius loop from Montornès del Vallès, a difficult 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route offers a substantial loop through the regional landscape, starting and ending in Montornès del Vallès. Local touring cyclists also love the Cornice Viewpoint – Gravel Parc loop from Montornès del Vallès, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Vilanova Del Vallès is defined by wooded slopes, the Mogent River basin, and hills within the Serralada Litoral Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Vilanova Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 11,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilanova Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: Touring cycling routes in Vilanova del Vallès offer a diverse landscape. You'll find routes traversing the Valles plain, featuring flatter sections along the Mogent River basin, as well as more undulating terrain in the foothills of the Litoral Mountain Range. A significant portion of the area is within the Serralada Litoral Park, characterized by wooded slopes with white pines and holm oaks, providing a mix of forest and agricultural lands.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes for different fitness levels in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: Yes, Vilanova del Vallès caters to various fitness levels. Out of nearly 300 touring cycling routes, you'll find options ranging from 36 easy routes, 98 moderate routes, to 159 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual riders and experienced cyclists can find suitable paths.

Q: What natural features can I explore while touring cycling in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Many routes pass through the Serralada Litoral Park, offering scenic wooded slopes. You can also experience the gentle ascent of the Parpers Pass or explore the Mogent River basin. For those seeking unique natural attractions, the Can Nadal Caves within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral are an interesting stop.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to visit on a touring cycling trip in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: Absolutely. The area is dotted with historical points of interest. You could cycle towards the nearly thousand-year-old &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1126577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, or visit the 11th-12th century Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3330797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Bartomeu de Cabanyes Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient megalithic monuments and other Romanesque constructions that can be integrated into your rides.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vilanova del Vallès are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural park settings, and the ability to combine physical activity with visits to historical sites and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for families in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes that could be suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33843153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flooded Pathway Through Reeds – Sant Agnès loop from Montornès del Vallès&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 18.2 km route with minimal elevation gain. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16353395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flooded Pathway Through Reeds loop from Montornès del Vallès&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.3 km long.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974261169/orrius-and-dolmen-de-can-buquet-circular-through-the-serralada-litoral-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Òrrius and Dolmen de Can Buquet - circular through the Serralada Litoral park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 25.1 km difficult route exploring the Serralada Litoral Park.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Vilanova del Vallès offers routes with substantial elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33911760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornice Viewpoint – Gravel Parc loop from Montornès del Vallès&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.4 km trail with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, leading through the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints accessible by touring bike in the region?
A: The area provides several rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas. Additionally, the Turó de Céllecs, the highest point in the municipality, provides expansive views of the surrounding landscape. The Gallzeran Tower is another challenging but rewarding destination for cycle tourists, offering views of the coast and Barcelona.

Q: Is it possible to combine touring cycling with visits to castles or historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region's touring cycling routes often pass near or lead to historical attractions. You can plan a ride that includes stops at sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1626785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Taió Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1982187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to enjoy both the physical activity and the cultural heritage of Vilanova del Vallès.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: There are nearly 300 touring cycling routes available around Vilanova del Vallès, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Vilanova del Vallès?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes and natural parks suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, and winter might be cooler, but generally, the Mediterranean climate allows for cycling year-round.
tips:
It is a very simple Romanesque-style hermitage, with a single nave covered with a barrel vault, with a semicircular apse, which has a double-flow window. The nave is very short, which suggests that it was shortened during the restoration,

We must highlight the arrangement of the belfry bell tower, perpendicular to the façade, for its originality. Next to the chapel is the hermit's house.

In the 15th century it was known as San Mateo del Bosque and was property of the Priory of Sant Miquel del Fai.

The chapel has been documented since the year 993. In a pastoral visit from 1498 it is recorded that it is in a ruinous state but it had to be restored shortly after since in the 16th century it had worship again.

In the 16th century restoration, the nave had to be cut off, now quite short in relation to the apse, and with a very simple façade.

On Saint Matthew's Day, its traditional pilgrimage is celebrated.

From the hermitage you can enjoy good views of the Maresme, an area frequented by several hiking and mountain biking routes.

La Roca d'en Toni is how this dolmen is known, which stands out for its good state of conservation. It is believed that it dates from between the third and second millennium BC and is currently one of the most important megalithic monuments in the Maresme.

Orrius is the smallest town in the Maresme and hides a disturbing forest. Probably the theme of the figures carved in stone is the most enigmatic of the Orrius Forest. It can be considered as a natural museum, if you apply a little imagination. Moai, Indian, elephant, strange inscriptions on rocks. You can talk about the stone as a substitute for the canvas.

It is a Mediterranean forest that is located in the municipality of Òrrius in which some large rocks are hidden in which different representations were carved.
It is not known who the authors were or were, but there are stories that relate it to mystical rites and it is even said that the forest is really enchanted and that strange voices can be heard, as well as seeing apparitions
